ogTitle: Dairy Plains vs Deddington: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Dairy Plains and Deddington.
heading: Dairy Plains vs Deddington — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Dairy Plains, Tasmania and Deddington, Tasmania. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Deddington has a lower overall climate risk score (30/100) compared to Dairy Plains (39/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Dairy Plains or Deddington safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Dairy Plains has a flood risk score of 56/100, while Deddington scores 23/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Dairy Plains scores 58/100 for bushfire risk. Deddington scores 48/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Dairy Plains or Deddington?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Deddington has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.37% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
